limitation: This is a heuristic scanner for agent review triage, not a security, performance, accessibility, design, or compatibility oracle. It can miss issues and produce false positives; verify findings manually against current WordPress docs, profiling, real UI review, third-party docs, and real integration tests.

findings.message: Output statement does not include an obvious escaping function.
findings.remediation: Escape late with esc_html(), esc_attr(), esc_url(), wp_kses(), or another context-appropriate escaping API.

findings.remediation: Escape late with esc_html(), esc_attr(), esc_url(), wp_kses(), or another context-appropriate escaping API.

findings.message: Dynamic block render output should be validated and escaped.
findings.remediation: Treat attributes as untrusted in render.php or render callbacks; validate, sanitize, authorize if needed, and escape late.

findings.message: package.json is missing scripts.lint:js.
findings.remediation: Add a lint:js script when the plugin ships block/editor JavaScript.

findings.message: package.json is missing a CSS/style lint script.
findings.remediation: Add lint:css or lint:style using wp-scripts lint-style when the plugin has CSS or SCSS.

findings.message: composer.json does not define a PHP lint script.
findings.remediation: Add a lint or lint:php script that runs PHPCS with WPCS.

findings.message: Code includes files from another plugin directory.
findings.why: Directly including third-party plugin files is fragile and can fatal when paths or versions change.
findings.remediation: Use feature detection and documented public APIs/hooks instead of requiring third-party plugin internals.

findings.message: Yoast SEO appears to be referenced without nearby feature detection.
findings.why: Optional integrations can fatal or misbehave when the plugin/theme/builder is inactive or changes APIs.
findings.remediation: Move integration code into an adapter and guard it with class_exists(), function_exists(), defined(), did_action(), or a documented detection method.

findings.message: Elementor appears to be referenced without nearby feature detection.
findings.why: Optional integrations can fatal or misbehave when the plugin/theme/builder is inactive or changes APIs.
findings.remediation: Move integration code into an adapter and guard it with class_exists(), function_exists(), defined(), did_action(), or a documented detection method.

findings.message: Elementor appears to be referenced without nearby feature detection.

findings.message: Classic Editor metabox save flow lacks an obvious nonce and capability check.
findings.why: Classic fallbacks must preserve the same security posture as block/editor saves.
findings.remediation: Add wp_verify_nonce()/check_admin_referer(), current_user_can(), sanitization, and escaping.

findings.message: TinyMCE/editor integration has no obvious screen or editor-context guard.
findings.why: Classic editor assets loaded globally can slow wp-admin and conflict with block editor screens.
findings.remediation: Gate TinyMCE assets by screen ID, post type, and editor context.

findings.message: SEO-relevant head output appears without an SEO plugin guard.
findings.why: Manual title, canonical, meta, social, or schema output can duplicate SEO plugin output.
findings.remediation: Add an SEO output guard, use documented SEO plugin hooks, and fallback only when no SEO plugin handles the concern.

findings.message: SEO-relevant output is not obviously filterable.
findings.why: SEO integrations often need project-specific overrides without editing plugin code.
findings.remediation: Expose documented filters around fallback SEO output and document when they run.

findings.message: Full cache purge appears in a normal request hook or broad callback.
findings.why: Purging all cache during normal requests can destroy site performance and conflict with cache plugins.
findings.remediation: Purge only affected URLs/posts on content/settings changes or explicit admin maintenance actions.

findings.message: Full cache purge appears in a normal request hook or broad callback.
findings.why: Purging all cache during normal requests can destroy site performance and conflict with cache plugins.
findings.remediation: Purge only affected URLs/posts on content/settings changes or explicit admin maintenance actions.

findings.message: Frontend output may include user-specific data without a cache compatibility note.
findings.why: User-specific data can leak through public page caches if not isolated.
findings.remediation: Separate private fragments, set no-cache behavior where appropriate, or document a private/ESI strategy.

findings.message: Asset or URL cache busting appears to use random/time-based values.
findings.why: Random query strings defeat browser/CDN/cache optimization and can fight performance plugins.
findings.remediation: Use plugin version, filemtime during development, or generated asset metadata.

findings.message: Frontend stylesheet contains broad/global selectors.
findings.why: Global CSS can break active themes, block themes, and page-builder layouts.
findings.remediation: Scope CSS under a plugin wrapper or block class and avoid resets.

findings.message: Stylesheet uses !important.
findings.why: Specificity fights are a common source of theme and builder conflicts.
findings.remediation: Reduce specificity conflicts through scoped selectors and component classes.

findings.message: Frontend stylesheet contains broad/global selectors.
findings.why: Global CSS can break active themes, block themes, and page-builder layouts.
findings.remediation: Scope CSS under a plugin wrapper or block class and avoid resets.

findings.message: Frontend stylesheet contains broad/global selectors.
findings.why: Global CSS can break active themes, block themes, and page-builder layouts.
findings.remediation: Scope CSS under a plugin wrapper or block class and avoid resets.

findings.message: Frontend stylesheet contains broad/global selectors.
findings.why: Global CSS can break active themes, block themes, and page-builder layouts.
findings.remediation: Scope CSS under a plugin wrapper or block class and avoid resets.

findings.message: Stylesheet uses !important.
findings.why: Specificity fights are a common source of theme and builder conflicts.
findings.remediation: Reduce specificity conflicts through scoped selectors and component classes.

findings.message: CSS uses a fixed pixel width.
findings.why: Fixed widths can break responsive themes and builder columns.
findings.remediation: Use max-width, minmax(), flex/grid, and logical properties where practical.

findings.message: Frontend markup has no obvious scoped wrapper class.
findings.why: Wrapper classes help scope CSS without overriding theme elements globally.
findings.remediation: Add a plugin or block wrapper class and keep markup semantic.

findings.message: Code appears to depend on theme files or paths.
findings.why: Plugins should not assume a theme file structure or edit/include theme internals.
findings.remediation: Use public hooks, templates, blocks, or documented theme APIs instead.

findings.message: Page-builder-specific code appears without a dependency guard.
findings.why: Builder adapters should not load when the builder is inactive.
findings.remediation: Move code into an optional adapter and detect Elementor/Divi through documented signals.

findings.message: Page-builder-specific code appears without a dependency guard.
findings.why: Builder adapters should not load when the builder is inactive.
findings.remediation: Move code into an optional adapter and detect Elementor/Divi through documented signals.

compatibility.limitation: Compatibility findings are static heuristics. They cannot prove support for real plugin/theme/builder versions; verify with current third-party docs and manual tests.
